title: Quadlet gets shut down when an automount volume reaches idle timeout
body:
### Issue Description

Please consider this quadlet:
```
# /etc/containers/systemd/test.container
[Unit]
Description=Container for testing
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target

[Container]
ContainerName=testing
Image=quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal:latest
Exec=/usr/bin/sleep 1h
HealthCmd=none
Volume=/mnt/nas/Media/All:/media

[Service]
# Restart service when sleep finishes
Restart=always
# Extend Timeout to allow time to pull the image
TimeoutStartSec=180

[Install]
# Start by default on boot
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```
With the volume defined in `/etc/fstab` as:
```
192.168.1.202:/volume1/Media /mnt/nas/Media	nfs4	x-systemd.automount,x-systemd.idle-timeout=300,async	0 0
```

When I start this quadlet, it gets shut down after 5 minutes, systematically. I'm getting this in the logs:
```
14:54:16 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Started test.service - Container for testing.
14:54:16 nuc.example.com test[170559]: 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82
14:59:16 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Stopping test.service - Container for testing...
14:59:26 nuc.example.com test[170777]: time="2023-12-16T14:59:26+01:00" level=warning msg="StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container testing in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL"
14:59:27 nuc.example.com podman[170777]: 2023-12-16 14:59:27.009535825 +0100 CET m=+10.132388112 container died 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82 (image=quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal:latest, name=testing, version=39, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=test.service, license=MIT, name=fedora, vendor=Fedora Project)
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82.timer: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Stopped 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82.timer - /usr/bin/podman healthcheck run 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com kernel: cni-podman0: port 1(veth7a1af3bc) entered disabled state
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: run-netns-netns\x2dfe8075ea\x2de3a9\x2d3626\x2d4e57\x2df4cde79b399a.mount: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay\x2dcontainers-0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82-userdata-shm.mount: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com podman[170777]: 2023-12-16 14:59:27.366781975 +0100 CET m=+10.489634302 container remove 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82 (image=quay.io/fedora/fedora-minimal:latest, name=testing, license=MIT, name=fedora, vendor=Fedora Project, version=39, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=test.service)
14:59:27 nuc.example.com test[170777]: 0d70a516d097571fd83c3789b77fe7b4432073dd926fc7a474f9bd1153f17e82
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: test.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=137/n/a
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: test.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Stopped test.service - Container for testing.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: test.service: Consumed 1.287s CPU time.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Unmounting mnt-nas-Media.mount - /mnt/nas/Media...
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay-7798aeea75b2376de27f0e017a3174d75fd28d1a88ec4ee751ac088b0d23244d-merged.mount: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: var-lib-containers-storage-overlay.mount: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: mnt-nas-Media.mount: Deactivated successfully.
14:59:27 nuc.example.com systemd[1]: Unmounted mnt-nas-Media.mount - /mnt/nas/Media.
```

The service receives a `stop` command exactly 5 minutes after its start, and since `/mnt/nas/Media` gets unmounted right after the stop and the automount timeout is 5 minutes, I think that's likely the culprit. If I remove the `Volume` line in the quadlet file, the service doesn't get stopped.

Is this a bug in Podman or is there something I must add to the `.container` file? (I didn't find anything about that in the docs)

I can easily reproduce it, so I'm happy to help debug it if needed.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Setup the provided quadlet
2. Setup the automount mount point
3. `systemctl daemon-reload` and start the automount mount point and the container
4. wait 5 minutes.


### Describe the results you received

The service gets stopped.

### Describe the results you expected

The automount mount point gets pinged (or however its timeout should get cancelled) and isn't unmounted, or it gets unmounted but is remounted transparently when the container accesses the volume.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fedora 39 freshly upgraded and updated.

### Additional information

_No response_
